document.title: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report For ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Test report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector covering MTF and DQE measurements under EN 62220-1-1.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory and non-clinical performance documentation for the ADD solid-state X-ray imaging detector configuration containing HAD1717MCW.
keyPoints: The report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices and includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.
The report states the tested configuration contained HAD1717MCW.
The device is not for fluoroscopic use and is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
The active area is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The report is EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report For ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-089 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-089 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The report was approved by the Quality Manager on Jun/7th/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Jun/7th/2021

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was established on Jun/5th/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Jun/5th/2021 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The report includes MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
evidence.evidenceText: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

evidence.claim: The ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Among the accessories of the ADD, the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The tested configuration in this report contained HAD1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: This report has been tested in a configuration containing HAD1717MCW.

evidence.claim: The applicable standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determination of detective quantum efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: The detector entire dimensions including outer short ring are 460 mm by 460 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Entire dimensions include outer short ring 460 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm

evidence.claim: The active pixel area size is 425.04 mm by 425.6 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Active pixel area size 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm

evidence.claim: The number of active area pixels is 3,036 x 3,040, totaling 9,229,440.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of active area pixel 3,036 x 3,040 = 9,229,440

evidence.claim: The scintillator is CsI and converts X-ray to visible light to charge the photodiode.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator CsI Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

evidence.claim: The detector frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate 1 fps

evidence.claim: Image data are transmitted and stored as 14-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 14-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two- dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The device is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.

evidence.claim: The DQE criteria for ADD are 0.1 lp/mm at at least 45%, 0.5 lp/mm at at least 40%, 1.0 lp/mm at at least 30%, 2.0 lp/mm at at least 25%, and 3.0 lp/mm at at least 20%.
Measured DQE for ADD was reported as 54.9% at 0.1 lp/mm, 52.6% at 0.5 lp/mm, 48.7% at 1.0 lp/mm, 33.7% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 22.9% at 3.0 lp/mm.
Aliasing artifacts are stated to be negligible because the MTF value at the sampling cut-off frequency is approximately close to zero.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm. The MTF value at this frequency is approximately close to zero. For average human observers, MTF values around 0.1 are considered barely observable. Therefore, artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible.

evidence.claim: The actual lag or residual signal level from prior exposures was 0.00015626.
evidence.evidenceText: Actual measurement: 0.00015626

evidence.claim: The device does not provide partial-area under-scanning functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: This device does not provide the applicable functionality.

evidence.claim: The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.

evidence.claim: Readout after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds before the detector is ready for the next exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after X-ray exposure requires at least 10 seconds at which time the detector is ready for the next exposure.

evidence.claim: Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Image readout, transferring and pre-processing requires under 5 seconds.

evidence.claim: The stability time is 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Stability time : 5 sec.

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The ADD meets regulatory standards as outlined in ADD safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337

evidence.claim: The ADD meets IEC standards as outlined in ADD safety report 397950 and EMC reports NK-20-E-0336 and NK-20-E-0337.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD meets the IEC standards as outlined in the ADD safety report: 397950, EMC report: NK-20-E-0336, NK-20-E-0337

evidence.claim: Linearity and sensitivity for ADD are reported as 189.9 LSB/uGy with equation 189.9x + 8.7265.
The reported MTF for HAD1717MCW is 96.4% at 0.1 lp, 83.2% at 0.5 lp, 62.1% at 1 lp, 29.8% at 2 lp, and 15.0% at 3 lp.
The reported DQE for HAD1717MCW is 50.9% at 0.1 lp, 44.6% at 0.5 lp, 42.7% at 1 lp, 33.7% at 2 lp, and 22.9% at 3 lp.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: metadata and source path signal FDA, but the document text does not explicitly identify FDA; it only references 21CFR1020.31 or .32.
Document date may be interpreted as approval date Jun/7th/2021 or revision establishment date Jun/5th/2021; approval date was used for documentDate.
Some extracted text appears to have OCR or preprocessing issues, including malformed units and punctuation such as '3..0 lp/mm' and '3..0 lp/mm(0 ≥ 22.9%)'.
Regulatory approvals and IEC standards are referenced through safety and EMC report numbers, but the specific regulatory standards and IEC standard numbers for those reports are not listed.
